differentials.name: Chronic thyroiditis Nearly 40% of patients with IgG4-related pancreatitis also have salivary ± lacrimal gland involvement, characterized by bilateral painless glandular swelling, which may precede / accompany pancreatitis / occur in isolation.
differentials.raw: Chronic thyroiditis Nearly 40% of patients with IgG4-related pancreatitis also have salivary ± lacrimal gland involvement, characterized by bilateral painless glandular swelling, which may precede / accompany pancreatitis / occur in isolation.

differentials.raw: no / mild upper abdominal pain (35%), easy fatigability

differentials.raw: bulging of papilla with obstructive jaundice (63-75%)

differentials.raw: symptoms of pancreatitis at presentation unusual (33%)

differentials.name: serum level of immunoglobulin G4
differentials.raw: serum level of immunoglobulin G4 (IgG4) > 130 mg/dL (67-94% sensitive, 90-100% specific)

differentials.name: positivity for ALA
differentials.raw: positivity for ALA (antilactoferrin antibody), ACA II (anti-carbonic anhydrase II antibody), ASMA (anti-smooth muscle antibody), ANA (antinuclear antibody)

differentials.name: Acute pancreatitis
differentials.raw: Acute pancreatitis (marked peripancreatic fat stranding, peripancreatic fat necrosis, NO peripancreatic halo)

differentials.name: symptoms of biliary obstruction (in 50%) rarely precede those of duodenal obstruction
differentials.raw: symptoms of biliary obstruction (in 50%) rarely precede those of duodenal obstruction

differentials.name: Pancreatic cancer: 50-70-fold increased risk within 7-30 years of disease onset ◊  Endoscopic US in 6-12-month intervals starting at 30 years of age Dx:  suspect after 2 attacks of acute pancreatitis without explanation like: anatomic anomalies, ampullary / MPD strictures, trauma, viral infection, gallstones, alcohol consumption, drug use, hyperlipidemia

context: Etiology: √  dilatation of main pancreatic duct √  normal sized / focally or diffusely enlarged / small atrophic gland √  calcifications uncommon Autoimmune Pancreatitis AIP (1.8-11%) =  PRIMARY / CHRONIC SCLEROSING PANCREATITIS = LYMPHOPLASMACYTIC SCLEROSING PANCREATITIS = NONALCOHOLIC DUCT-DESTRUCTIVE / IDIOPATHIC DUCT-CENTRIC CHRONIC PANCREATITIS = PSEUDOTUMOROUS PANCREATITIS =  unique form of chronic pancreatitis characterized by diffuse inflammatory infiltration by T-lymphocytes and IgG4-positive plasma cells with exuberant fibrosis Histo:  dense periductal lymphoplasmacytic infiltrates T
